description: Content-hash algorithm registry. Each identifier names a hash function used to commit to content in a record's `hashes` map and to a Merkle leaf's content digest. Both registered identifiers are MANDATORY-to-implement, so a single-hash record under either validates on every conformant implementation. The pinned `digest_length_bytes` is the exact byte length the structural validator requires for a digest under that identifier; any other length is a HASH_DIGEST_LENGTH_MISMATCH, and any unregistered identifier is an UNSUPPORTED_HASH_ALG (rejected, never silently skipped — the content hash is load-bearing for the claim). v1 scope: only 32-byte-digest algorithms may be added without a schema bump, because the grammar pins 32-byte digests; a different digest length requires a successor revision with a top-level `v` bump. Additions are additive-only and must ship with conformance vectors.

entries.description: SHA-256 from the SHA-2 family. The interoperability baseline content hash.
BLAKE2b with a 256-bit output. A fast modern alternative to SHA-256 for content commitments.
entries.reference: RFC 7693 (The BLAKE2 Cryptographic Hash and Message Authentication Code)
